description: Real adapter implementing OptolinkPort using pyserial and the vito2mqtt.optolink sub-package. Connect-per-cycle: each read/write creates a serial connection, initialises communication, executes the command, and disconnects. Thread-safe via asyncio lock. Lazy import of pyserial.
title: Implement OptolinkAdapter (real serial)

description: Clean-room TDD: Spec agent writes behavioral specs + tests for the Optolink session controller. Covers: initialization handshake (reset → sync → ack), read command execution (send telegram → ack → response → parse), write command execution with payload, reconnection on error, access mode validation. Connect-per-cycle pattern.
title: Spec: Optolink session controller tests

description: Create foundational ADRs for the vito2mqtt greenfield build. These decisions establish the architectural direction before implementation begins.
title: Architectural Decision Records

description: Define OptolinkPort as a PEP 544 @runtime_checkable Protocol. Methods: read_signal(name) → decoded value, write_signal(name, value), read_signals(names) → dict of values. The port abstracts away all protocol details — callers use English signal names and typed Python values.
title: Define OptolinkPort protocol (PEP 544)

close_reason: 18 integration tests merged via PR #21: lifecycle (5), commands (8), error isolation (5). Shared helpers in conftest, TOPIC_PREFIX constant.
description: Integration tests using cosalette AppHarness: full lifecycle (startup → telemetry → shutdown), command dispatch, error isolation. Use FakeOptolinkAdapter. Verify MQTT message publication and topic correctness.
title: Integration tests with AppHarness

description: Implement _run_telemetry_group() with priority-queue algorithm and integer-millisecond tick arithmetic. Update _start_device_tasks() to partition registrations by group. Per-handler error isolation. Init function handling with exclusion on failure. See docs/planning/coalescing-groups-framework-requirements.md §4.
title: Phase 2: Tick-aligned scheduler core

description: Async device function registered via @app.device('legionella'). State machine: idle->checking->heating->restoring->idle. Command handler accepts {action: start|cancel} on legionella/set. Publishes status to legionella/state (status, remaining_minutes, original_setpoint, reason). Uses ctx.sleep() for duration countdown. Reads timer_hw_{weekday} for safety check. Writes hot_water_setpoint to configurable legionella_temperature (default 68) on start, restores original on end/cancel (immediate restore on cancel). Persists original setpoint via cosalette Store for crash recovery. register_legionella(app) function.
title: Legionella device state machine + registration

close_reason: Implemented via feat/configurable-store-path branch: XDG-based store path resolution + Docker volume + docs
description: The hardcoded relative path `JsonFileStore("vito2mqtt_store.json")` in main.py should be configurable. Consider using `XDG_STATE_HOME` or a cosalette lifespan hook for proper path resolution. Currently the store file is written relative to the working directory, which is fragile in deployment.
title: Make device store path configurable (XDG_STATE_HOME)

description: Build the cosalette application layer: settings, telemetry devices (domain-grouped), command handlers, app assembly, lifespan, error types.
title: Application Layer (cosalette devices)

description: Clean-room TDD: Impl agent implements Vitodens 200-W command registry from spec/tests only. Module: vito2mqtt.optolink.commands.
title: Impl: Vitodens 200-W command registry

description: Add cosalette and pyserial as project dependencies. Set up the vito2mqtt.optolink sub-package directory structure with __init__.py files. Ensure uv sync and tests pass.
title: Add cosalette dep + optolink pkg scaffold

description: Fake adapter implementing OptolinkPort for dry-run mode and testing. Returns realistic-looking static/cycling values. No hardware dependency. Supports configurable response values for test scenarios.
title: Implement FakeOptolinkAdapter (dry-run)

description: MQTT signal reference: complete list of all published MQTT topics, their payload schemas, polling intervals, and descriptions. Organised by domain entity (outdoor, hot_water, burner, heating_radiator, heating_floor, system, diagnosis). Include writable signals and command payload formats.
title: Docs: MQTT signal reference

description: ADR: Implement the Viessmann Optolink P300 protocol as an internal sub-package (vito2mqtt.optolink). Covers transport layer, telegram framing, data type codec, and Vitodens 200-W command registry. Clean-room TDD approach for implementation. English signal names throughout.
title: ADR-004: Optolink Protocol Design

description: Integration tests use asyncio.sleep() for timing (0.3s–0.4s waits). Review whether event-driven synchronisation (e.g. waiting for a specific publish count) would make tests faster and less flaky. Gate task: evaluate before adding more integration tests.
title: Review sleep-based timing in integration tests

description: register_telemetry() and register_commands() access app.settings at import time (module-level in main.py). If VITO2MQTT_SERIAL_PORT is not set, importing vito2mqtt.main raises RuntimeError before the CLI can parse --help or --version. This is a framework-level concern: cosalette's add_telemetry(interval=...) requires a concrete value at declaration time, preventing lazy settings resolution. Options: (1) cosalette supports deferred interval via callable, (2) register handlers in a startup hook if cosalette allows late registration, (3) accept the constraint and document it. Discovered during PR #12 review.
title: CLI --help/--version fails without VITO2MQTT_SERIAL_PORT
DRY: extract shared _lookup method from serial.py and fake.py

description: Implement legionella treatment as a cosalette @app.device. Raises hot water setpoint to 68°C for a configurable duration (default 40 min) to kill legionella bacteria. Controllable via MQTT (start/cancel). Safety check: must be within boiler hot water heating interval with sufficient margin. Persists original setpoint for crash recovery. Replaces legacy OpenHab rule (docs/planning/legacy/openhab.rule).
title: Legionella treatment function

description: Create the documentation landing page (docs/index.md) and navigation structure in zensical.toml. User-journey sections: Get Started, Install/Setup, Use (common flows), Reference. Ensure GitHub Pages deployment works.
title: Docs: Landing page + nav structure

description: Evaluate whether OptolinkAdapter should keep P300 session open across sequential batch calls within a coalescing group tick. Out of scope for ADR-007 — the framework guarantees sequential execution; adapter decides session reuse. Revisit after Phase 4 is complete. See docs/planning/coalescing-groups-framework-requirements.md §5, Q3.
title: Gate: Adapter session optimization (deferred)

description: Clean-room TDD: Spec agent writes behavioral specs + tests for data type codec. Covers all Vitodens 200-W types: IS10 (signed/10), IUNON (unsigned), IU3600 (seconds to hours), BA (operating mode enum), RT (return status), CT (cycle timer bit-packing), ES (error code + timestamp), TI (system time BCD), PR2 (second-byte extraction), PR3 (first-byte /2), USV (switch valve enum). Both encoding (value→bytes) and decoding (bytes→value).
title: Spec: Data type codec tests

description: ADR: Use hexagonal architecture with PEP 544 Protocol ports for hardware abstraction. OptolinkPort as the primary port. Connect-per-cycle strategy for serial sessions (not persistent). Adapter lifecycle via lifespan context manager. Dry-run mode with FakeOptolinkAdapter.
title: ADR-003: Hardware Abstraction (Hexagonal)

description: Define domain-specific error types: OptolinkConnectionError, OptolinkTimeoutError, InvalidReadingError, CommandNotWritableError. Create error_type_map for cosalette error publishing.
title: Define domain error types

close_reason: Integrated group=optolink in register_telemetry, bumped cosalette to >=0.1.6
description: Update register_telemetry() to pass group='optolink' to all 7 handlers. Bump cosalette dependency to >=0.2.0. Update tests. Verify session coalescing. See docs/planning/coalescing-groups-framework-requirements.md §11.
title: Phase 4: vito2mqtt integration (group=optolink)

description: Clean-room TDD: Spec agent writes behavioral specs + tests for the Vitodens 200-W command registry. Covers: command lookup by English name, address/length/unit/access_mode resolution, reverse lookup from address bytes. All signals from the Vitodens 200-W command set with English names. Read/write access mode enforcement.
title: Spec: Vitodens 200-W command registry tests

description: Implement the hexagonal adapter layer: OptolinkPort protocol, real serial adapter, and fake adapter for dry-run/testing.
title: Adapter Layer (Ports & Adapters)

description: Float precision tests (300x12==3600). Shutdown between handlers in batch. Integration tests with AppHarness + FakeClock. Multiple groups simultaneously. Update cosalette documentation. See docs/planning/coalescing-groups-framework-requirements.md §10.
title: Phase 3: Edge cases, integration tests, docs

description: Add legionella_temperature (default 68), legionella_duration_minutes (default 40), and legionella_safety_margin_minutes (default 30) to Vito2MqttSettings. Wire register_legionella(app) into main.py composition root. Integration-level tests with FakeOptolinkAdapter covering full start->heat->restore cycle, cancel mid-treatment, safety check rejection, and crash recovery (persisted setpoint restore on startup).
title: Legionella config + composition root wiring

description: Clean-room TDD implementation of the P300 Optolink protocol for Vitodens 200-W communication. Spec agent writes tests from behavioral specs; impl agent implements without viewing legacy code.
title: Optolink Protocol Layer (Clean-Room TDD)

description: ADR: Choose cosalette as the application framework. Decision drivers: composition root pattern, async MQTT lifecycle, decorator-based device API, hexagonal architecture support, built-in health/availability, testing infrastructure (AppHarness, fixtures). Alternatives: raw paho-mqtt, fastapi+mqtt, custom framework.
title: ADR-001: Framework Choice (cosalette)

description: Change AccessMode.WRITE → READ_WRITE for all 42 writable signals (legacy analysis confirms bidirectional access at same address). Add read-before-write logic in command handlers: read current value, compare, skip write if equal. Add __force flag to bypass comparison. Coordinate with TI encoding (system_time). Requires planning doc. Legacy evidence: docs/planning/legacy/pyvcontrol/ — test_writesequence confirms read→write→read-back cycle at real hardware.
title: Read-before-write optimization for command handlers

description: ADR: Define MQTT topic layout using domain-entity grouping. Topics: vito2mqtt/{device}/state for telemetry groups (outdoor, hot_water, burner, heating_radiator, heating_floor, system, diagnosis), vito2mqtt/{device}/set for writable parameters. JSON payloads with English keys. Home Assistant compatible.
title: ADR-002: MQTT Topic Layout (domain-grouped)

description: Getting Started section: prerequisites (Python 3.14, Optolink adapter), installation via uv/pip, minimal .env configuration, first run with --dry-run, verifying MQTT output.
title: Docs: Getting Started guide

description: Clean-room TDD: Impl agent implements Optolink session controller from spec/tests only. Module: vito2mqtt.optolink.transport. Integrates telegram, codec, and commands modules. Connect-per-cycle serial sessions.
title: Impl: Optolink session controller

description: Configuration reference: all settings with defaults, .env examples, environment variable reference (VITO2MQTT_ prefix), polling interval tuning guide.
title: Docs: Configuration reference

description: Create comprehensive Zensical-based user-journey documentation for vito2mqtt, deployed as GitHub Pages. Coverage: getting started, installation, usage, reference, ADR index.
title: Documentation (Zensical / GitHub Pages)

description: Create Vito2MqttSettings subclass of cosalette.Settings. Fields: serial_port (default /dev/ttyOpto), baud_rate (4800), polling intervals per domain group (outdoor_interval, hot_water_interval, burner_interval, heating_radiator_interval, heating_floor_interval, system_interval, diagnosis_interval) with sensible defaults. Env prefix VITO2MQTT_.
title: Implement Vito2MqttSettings

description: Clean-room TDD: Impl agent implements P300 telegram framing from the spec/tests only, without viewing legacy code. Module: vito2mqtt.optolink.telegram.
title: Impl: P300 telegram framing

description: New module devices/legionella.py with pure function is_within_heating_window(schedule: CycleTimeSchedule, now: time, safety_margin_minutes: int) -> bool. Maps day-of-week to timer_hw_{day} signal names. Fully unit-testable with no I/O. Edge cases: within window, outside window, near end (within margin), empty schedule, None slots, midnight-crossing windows.
title: Heating window check module (pure logic)

description: Implement @app.telemetry devices for all 7 domain groups: outdoor, hot_water, burner, heating_radiator, heating_floor, system, diagnosis. Each reads its signal set via OptolinkPort and returns a JSON-serialisable dict with English keys. Configurable intervals from settings.
title: Implement telemetry devices (all 7 groups)

description: Docker deployment: Dockerfile, docker-compose.yml (with serial device pass-through and Mosquitto), .env example for container deployments. CI pipeline updates for building and publishing the Docker image.
title: Docker deployment config

description: Include ADRs in the documentation site. Add ADR index page with status overview. Link from nav to docs/adr/ directory.
title: Docs: ADR index in docs site

description: Clean-room TDD: Impl agent implements data type codec from spec/tests only. Module: vito2mqtt.optolink.codec.
title: Impl: Data type codec

description: Add group: str | None = None to _TelemetryRegistration, telemetry() decorator, and add_telemetry(). Validate empty string. Thread to registration constructor. All existing tests must pass. See docs/planning/coalescing-groups-framework-requirements.md §3, §2.
title: Phase 1: Data model + API surface (group= param)

description: Integration tests, Docker deployment config, CI pipeline updates, and final polish.
title: Integration & Deployment

description: ADR: Configuration management using cosalette.Settings subclass with pydantic-settings. Env var prefix VITO2MQTT_, .env file support, configurable polling intervals with sensible defaults (outdoor: 300s, burner: 300s, hot_water: 300s, heating_radiator: 300s, heating_floor: 300s, system: 3600s, diagnosis: 300s), serial port path, baud rate.
title: ADR-005: Configuration & Settings

close_reason: All writable parameter command handlers implemented (4 groups, 43 signals)
description: Implement @app.command handlers for writable parameters: hot_water (setpoint, pump lag, timers), heating_radiator (curve gradient/level, room temps, op modes, timers), heating_floor (same as radiator for M2), system (system time). Validate incoming payloads, dispatch writes via OptolinkPort.
title: Implement command handlers (writable params)

close_reason: Implemented command handler factory, register_commands, and comprehensive tests
title: Phase 2: Command handler factory + registration

close_reason: All 4 implementation phases complete (upstream in cosalette 0.1.6, integration done). Gate task workspace-6xf remains open as deferred P3.
description: Implement coalescing groups in cosalette: group= parameter on telemetry API, tick-aligned scheduler, per-handler error isolation. ADR-007. Requirements: docs/planning/coalescing-groups-framework-requirements.md
title: Coalescing Groups (cosalette framework)

description: Usage guides: Home Assistant integration (MQTT auto-discovery or manual config), Docker deployment, monitoring/troubleshooting, common recipes (change heating schedule, adjust hot water temperature).
title: Docs: Usage guides + Home Assistant

description: Clean-room TDD: Spec agent writes behavioral specs + tests for P300 telegram framing. Covers: start byte 0x41, header (type, mode), address + data length, payload, checksum calculation (sum mod 256). Supports request/response/error types and read/write/call modes. Parsing from bytes and construction from components.
title: Spec: P300 telegram framing tests

description: Wire the cosalette App composition root in app.py: create App instance with settings_class, register OptolinkPort adapter (with lazy import for real, FakeOptolinkAdapter for dry-run), attach all telemetry and command devices, add app.run() entry point.
title: App assembly (composition root)
Optimize read_signals to use single serial session for batch reads
